1Selekt — on the design language

Rebuilt on DESIGN.md. Dark screening-room theme; neon is light, not paint (electric magenta = the live signal, rationed). Two temperatures: resting screens read like a black-and-white art film; live screens are an LA night. Archivo for title-cards, Switzer for UI, Source Serif for Liner Notes — the curator's voice, JetBrains Mono for timecode. The Room is explored three ways (1a–1c); live beats in 1d; resting surfaces 1e–1j.

1a The Room — Liner Notes stream

devin
↓ New note
Naomi · hosting
142 in the room
react

Liner Notes are the center of gravity — serif, the curator's voice, ~70% of the view. The "now" note carries the one permitted neon tick. Room chatter sits typographically beneath it.

1b The Room — one note at a time

the room · 34

Made for the second glance in a dark room. One note fills the frame; chat and reactions collapse to the thumb zone. Quietest possible companion to the film.

The room talks; the curator's notes still win the eye — bigger, serif, ticked. Most communal of the three, at the cost of pulling attention off the TV.

④ Reel change (resync + away)
9:10selekt▮▮
Welcome back
you stepped out — 2 notes since
0:52

0:59

you've drifted 40s behind the room
Resync

The signature is T-0 (a hard cut to night + the ON AIR sign igniting). Notes fade up like subtitles. Intermission is the lobby — mono numerals, no glow, beautiful and empty. Desync is never a failure, it's a reel change.

At rest the app is matte, typographic, off-white on warm near-black — a monograph. The only neon is the primary button ("the neon sign"), unglowed because nothing is live yet.

1f The marquee — resting, with live cards igniting

The marquee

ON AIR NOW

In the Mood for Love
"The most romantic film about not touching ever made." — Naomi
34 going · 3 friendsReserve
THIS WEEK
Paris, Texas
SAT 8PM · LEO
remind
MarqueeRoster◎NotesYou

A lineup, not a scroll — everything anchored to a start time. Resting rows are matte; the "on air now" cards carry glass sheen and the glow that means this is happening right now.

1g Session detail — reserve a seat
5:22▮▮
one-sheet
In the Mood for Love
2000 · 1H38M · HOSTED BY NAOMI
TONIGHT · 8:00PMin 2h 40m
WHY NAOMI PICKED IT
I come back to this one every year. It's about the space between two people — and how much a hallway can hold.
MAKE SURE YOU CAN PLAY IT
▶ MUBIrent $3.99
THE ROOM
34 · incl. 3 friends
Reserve a seat

Reserving is the commitment device — a shared 8pm only works if everyone opts in. The neon button stays unglowed until T-0, when it becomes "Take your seat" and ignites.

RATE IT★ ★ ★ ★ ☆
Naomi's next session · Sat 8pm

The live moment becomes a keepable artifact. Film DNA is discovery, not liveness — so it stays fully matte, no neon: hairline nodes, the watched film ringed in off-white.

1i Taste Passport — a library card, no neon
5:25▮▮
Sam R.
@SAMR · 24 CURATORS · 38 SESSIONS
THE PASSPORT
RECENT SESSIONS
PROOF YOU WERE IN THE ROOM
opening night · Vidiots10 sessions running
MarqueeRoster◎NotesYou

You are what you showed up for. Strict Letterboxd-style four-up contact sheet, hairline borders, fully matte — a collectible library card, never a gamer profile. No neon anywhere.

② Research agent
7:41selekt · host▮▮
Research agent
Brief it, then let it dig.
SCOPE
locationsabove the linebelow the linecastperiod & context
PROMPT
Dig into In the Mood for Love (2000, Wong Kar-wai). Shooting locations and how to spot them — Hong Kong tenements, Bangkok stand-ins, Angkor Wat. The craft: Doyle & Mark Lee Ping-bing's photography, William Chang's design and the cheongsams. Cast: Maggie Cheung, Tony Leung. The 1962 Shanghainese-diaspora Hong Kong it lives in, and the Nat King Cole cues. Return screening-ready findings.
Run deep research
③ Findings → note
7:43selekt · host▮▮
Findings
Highlight a line to make it a note.
BELOW THE LINE
The cheongsams change in nearly every scene — William Chang uses them as a clock and a mood ring.
add as note ›
LOCATIONS
The cramped hallways were shot in a Bangkok building — Hong Kong had nothing left that old.
SOUND
"Quizás, Quizás, Quizás" recurs like a question neither of them answers.

7:45selekt · host▮▮

⑦ On air
8:47selekt · host▮▮
ON AIR0:46 · 142 seated
◆ NOTES FIRING AUTOMATICALLY
NEXT
Watch the cheongsams — a clock and a mood ring.
fires in 0:38
+ drop a note now…
group pauseintermissionTelestrator
End session

The whole craft, end to end: brief a research agent on the film's locations, craft, cast and period → highlight any finding into a note → pin it to a timestamp and attach a visual → the queue is yours, not auto-generated. Press play in sync with the TV and every note fires itself off that clock. Live, the curator mostly watches — dropping the occasional note by hand.

Try next: “take 1a to hi-fi with a real still” · “try the T-0 going-live ignition as its own screen” · “make the marquee 1f denser” · “show the New Wave tricolor Howl share card” · “dial the neon back further on 1f”.
